title: लक्ष्मीनृसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मीनृसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्रम् ॥

श्रीमत्पयोनिधिनिकेतनचक्रपाणे
भोगीन्द्रभोगमणिराजितपुण्यमूर्ते।
योगीश शाश्वत शरण्य भवाब्धिपोत
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ १॥

ब्रह्मेन्द्ररुद्रमरुदर्ककिरीटकोटि-
संघट्टिताङ्घ्रिकमलामलकान्तिकान्त।
लक्ष्मीलसत्कुचसरोरुहराजहंस
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ २॥

संसारदावदहनाकरभीकरोरु-
ज्वालावलीभिरतिदग्धतनूरुहस्य।
त्वत्पादपद्मसरसीरुहमागतस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ३॥

संसारजालपतितस्य जगन्निवास
सर्वेन्द्रियार्थबडिशाग्रझषोपमस्य।
प्रोत्कम्पितप्रचुरतालुकमस्तकस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ४॥

संसारकूपमतिघोरमगाधमूलं
सम्प्राप्य दुःखशतसर्पसमाकुलस्य।
दीनस्य देव कृपया पदमागतस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ५॥

संसारभीकरकरीन्द्रकराभिघात-
निष्पीड्यमानवपुषः सकलार्तिनाश।
प्राणप्रयाणभवभीतिसमाकुलस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ६॥

संसारसर्पविषदिग्धमहोग्रतीव्रं
दंष्ट्राग्रकोटिपरिदष्टविनष्टमूर्तेः।
नागारिवाहन सुधाब्धिनिवास शौरे
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ७॥

संसारवृक्षमघबीजमनन्तकर्म-
शाखाशतं करणपत्रमनङ्गपुष्पम्।
आरुह्य दुःखफलितं चकितं दयालो
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ८॥

संसारसागरविशालकरालकाल-
नक्रग्रहग्रसितनिग्रहविग्रहस्य।
व्यग्रस्य रागरसनोर्मिनिपीडितस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ९॥

संसारसागरनिमज्जनमुह्यमानं
दीनं विलोकय विभो करुणानिधे माम्।
प्रह्लादखेदपरिहारपरावतार
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ १०॥

बद्ध्वा गले यमभटा बहु तर्जयन्तः
कर्षन्ति यत्र भवपाशशतैर्यतन्तम्।
एकाकिनं परवशं चकितं दयालो
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ ११॥

लक्ष्मीपते कमलनाभ सुरेश विष्णो
यज्ञेश यज्ञ मधुसूदन विश्वरूप।
ब्रह्मण्य केशव जनार्दन वासुदेव
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ १२॥

एकेन चक्रमपरेण करेण शंख-
मन्येन सिन्धुतनयामवलम्ब्य तिष्ठन्।
वामेतरेण वरदाभयपद्मचिह्नं
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ १३॥

अन्धस्य मे हृतविवेकमहाधनस्य
चोरैर्महाबलिभिरिन्द्रियनामधेयैः।
मोहान्धकारकुहरे विनिपातितस्य
लक्ष्मीनृसिंह मम देहि करावलम्बम्॥ १४॥

प्रह्लादनारदपराशरपुण्डरीक-
व्यासाम्बरीषशुकशौनकभीष्मदाल्भ्यान्।
रुक्माङ्गदार्जुनवसिष्ठविभीषणादी-
न् पुण्यानिमान् परमभागवतान् स्मरामि॥ १५॥

लक्ष्मीनृसिंहचरणाब्जमधुव्रतेन
स्तोत्रं कृतं शुभकरं भुवि शंकरेण।
ये तत्पठन्ति मनुजा हरिभक्तियुक्ताः
ते यान्ति तत्पदसरोजमखण्डरूपम्॥ १६॥

॥ इति श्रीमत्परमहंसपरिव्राजकाचार्यस्य श्रीगोविन्दभगवत्पूज्यपाद-
शिष्यस्य श्रीमच्छंकरभगवतः कृतौ
श्री लक्ष्मीनृसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram ||

Shrimat-payo-nidhi-niketana-chakra-pane
Bhogi-indra-bhoga-mani-rajita-punya-murte.
Yogisha shashvata sharanya bhava-abdhi-pota
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 1 ||

Brahma-indra-rudra-marud-arka-kirita-koti-
Sanghatita-anghri-kamala-amala-kanti-kanta.
Lakshmi-lasat-kucha-saroruha-raja-hamsa
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 2 ||

Samsara-dava-dahana-akara-bhikara-uru-
Jvala-avalibhir-ati-dagdha-tanu-uruhasya.
Tvat-pada-padma-sarasiruh-amagatasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 3 ||

Samsara-jala-patitasya jagan-nivasa
Sarva-indriya-artha-badisha-agra-jhasho-pamasya.
Protkampita-prachura-taluka-mastakasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 4 ||

Samsara-kupam-ati-ghoram-agadha-mulam
Samprapya duhkha-shata-sarpa-samakulasya.
Dinasya Deva kripaya padam-agatasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 5 ||

Samsara-bhikara-karindra-kara-abhighata-
Nishpidya-mana-vapushah sakala-arti-nasha.
Prana-prayana-bhava-bhiti-samakulasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 6 ||

Samsara-sarpa-visha-digdha-maha-ugra-tivram
Damshtra-agra-koti-paridashta-vinashta-murteh.
Naga-ari-vahana sudha-abdhi-nivasa Shaure
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 7 ||

Samsara-vriksham-agha-bijam-ananta-karma-
Shakha-shatam karana-patram-ananga-pushpam.
Aruhya duhkha-phalitam chakitam dayalo
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 8 ||

Samsara-sagara-vishala-karala-kala-
Nakra-graha-grasita-nigraha-vigrahasya.
Vyagrasya raga-rasana-urmi-nipiditasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 9 ||

Samsara-sagara-nimajjana-muhyamanam
Dinam vilokaya Vibho karuna-nidhe mam.
Prahlada-kheda-parihara-para-avatara
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 10 ||

Baddhva gale yama-bhata bahu tarjayantah
Karshanti yatra bhava-pasha-shatair-yatantam.
Ekakinam para-vasham chakitam dayalo
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 11 ||

Lakshmi-pate kamala-nabha suresha Vishno
Yajnesha yajna Madhusudana vishva-rupa.
Brahmanya Keshava Janardana Vasudeva
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 12 ||

Ekena chakram-aparena karena shankham-
Anyena sindhu-tanayam-avalambya tishthan.
Vama-itarena varada-abhaya-padma-chihnam
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 13 ||

Andhasya me hrita-viveka-maha-dhanasya
Choraih-maha-balibhir-indriya-nama-dheyaih.
Moha-andhakara-kuhare vinipatitasya
Lakshmi-Nrisimha mama dehi karavalambam. || 14 ||

Prahlada-Narada-Parashara-Pundarika-
Vyasa-Ambarisha-Shuka-Shaunaka-Bhishma-Dalbhyan.
Rukmangada-Arjuna-Vasishtha-Vibhishana-adin
Punyan-iman parama-bhagavatan smarami. || 15 ||

Lakshmi-Nrisimha-charana-abja-madhu-vratena
Stotram kritam shubha-karam bhuvi Shankarena.
Ye tat-pathanti manuja Hari-bhakti-yuktah
Te yanti tat-pada-sarojam-akhanda-rupam. || 16 ||

|| Iti Shrimat-Paramahamsa-Parivrajakacharya Shri Govinda-Bhagavat-Pujyapada-
Shishyasya Shrimach-Shankara-Bhagavatah Kritau
Shri Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे क्षीरसागर निवासी, चक्रपाणि, शेषनाग के मणियों से विराजित पुण्यमूर्ति, योगीश, शाश्वत, शरण्य, संसार-सागर के नौका! हे लक्ष्मीनृसिंह! मुझे अपने हाथ का सहारा दीजिए॥ १॥

ब्रह्मा, इन्द्र, रुद्र, मरुत, सूर्य — इनके मुकुटों की कोटियों से टकराने वाले चरणकमल वाले, निर्मल कान्ति से कान्त, लक्ष्मी के कुच-सरोवर के राजहंस! हे लक्ष्मीनृसिंह! मुझे करावलम्ब दीजिए॥ २॥

संसार रूपी दावाग्नि की भयंकर ज्वालाओं से दग्ध शरीर वाले मेरा, जो आपके चरणकमल-सरोवर में आया हूँ — हे लक्ष्मीनृसिंह! मुझे करावलम्ब दीजिए॥ ३॥

संसार रूपी अत्यन्त भयंकर, अगाध कूप में गिरे हुए, सैकड़ों दुःख रूपी सर्पों से व्याकुल, दीन, आपके चरणों में आये हुए मेरी — हे देव! कृपा करें — हे लक्ष्मीनृसिंह! करावलम्ब दीजिए॥ ५॥

संसार रूपी वृक्ष — पाप बीज, अनन्त कर्म शाखाएँ, इन्द्रिय पत्ते, काम पुष्प — इस पर चढ़कर दुःख फल पाने वाला, भयभीत — हे दयालु! करावलम्ब दीजिए॥ ८॥

हे करुणानिधे विभो! संसार-सागर में डूबते हुए दीन मुझे देखिए। प्रह्लाद के कष्ट निवारण के लिए अवतरित — हे लक्ष्मीनृसिंह! करावलम्ब दीजिए॥ १०॥

लक्ष्मीनृसिंह के चरणकमल के मधुव्रत (भ्रमर) शंकर ने यह शुभकारी स्तोत्र रचा। जो मनुष्य हरिभक्ति से युक्त होकर इसे पढ़ते हैं, वे उनके अखण्ड चरणकमल को प्राप्त करते हैं॥ १६॥
benefits: One of the most famous stotras by Adi Shankaracharya
Each verse is a cry for divine hand (karavalamba) from worldly suffering
Invokes immediate protection and relief from samsara's afflictions
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of death, disease, and yama's messengers
Combines the grace of both Lakshmi and Narasimha
Extremely powerful for protection against all forms of danger
Recitation leads to the lotus feet of the Lord (moksha)
description: Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram (लक्ष्मीनृसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Narasimha. Attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to combine the grace of both Lakshmi and Narasimha as well as recitation leads to the lotus feet of the Lord (moksha). Additionally, it is known to destroy fear of death, disease, and Yama's messengers. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar), Saturday (Shanivar), and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 16 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Famous prayer seeking the divine hand of Lord Narasimha.

Karavalambam meaning hand of refuge support

Best Adi Shankara stotras complete list
Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba audio MP3 chant
Ahobilam Lakshmi Narasimha temple Andhra Pradesh
Narasimha for protection from yama Kala bhaya
Samsara dava dahana fire of worldly suffering

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram (श्री लक्ष्मीनृसिंह करावलम्ब स्तोत्रम्) is one of the most beloved devotional compositions of Adi Shankaracharya (8th century CE), comprising sixteen verses in the elegant Vasanta-tilaka chhanda. Each verse is a heartfelt cry to Lord Lakshmi-Narasimha — Vishnu's man-lion avatara accompanied by his consort Lakshmi — and ends with the refrain 'Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam' meaning 'O Lakshmi-Narasimha, give me the support of your hand'. The colophon explicitly attributes the text to Shri Govinda Bhagavatpada-shishya Shankara Bhagavat — confirming Adi Shankara as the author. The stotra metaphorically describes samsara as a forest fire, a deep well, a venomous serpent, a destructive elephant and a poison-tree, from which only Narasimha's outstretched hand can rescue the devotee. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya; classical Vaishnava nityapaath tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Karavalambam in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Karavalambam (कर + अवलम्बम्) is a beautiful Sanskrit compound meaning 'support of the hand' — kara = hand, avalambam = support, refuge, holding-up. It evokes the image of a drowning person reaching up and crying out for the Lord to extend his hand and pull them up out of the ocean of samsara. The same construction appears in two other famous Karavalamba stotras of Adi Shankara — the Devi Karavalamba and the Krishna Karavalamba — making this a signature genre of Shankara's bhakti compositions. The repeated refrain 'Lakshmi-Nrisimha Mama Dehi Karavalambam' — give me, O Lakshmi-Narasimha, the support of your hand — is one of the most plaintive and beloved bhakti pleas in all Sanskrit literature. (Sources: Adi Shankara karavalamba-stotra genre; Sanskrit etymology.)

seoFaqs.question: What metaphors does the stotram use to describe samsara?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram uses a series of vivid samsara metaphors that have become classical in Vaishnava bhakti literature. Verse 3 calls samsara a samsara-dava-dahana — a forest fire whose flames burn the body's hair-tips. Verse 4 calls it a samsara-jala — a fishing net in which the devotee is hooked like a fish on the bait of sense-objects. Verse 5 calls it a samsara-kupa — a deep, fathomless well filled with hundreds of suffering-serpents. Verse 6 calls it a samsara-bhikara-karindra — a terrifying rogue elephant crushing the body with its trunk. Verse 7 calls it a samsara-sarpa — a venomous serpent whose poison-tipped fangs destroy the body. Verse 8 calls it a samsara-vriksha — a tree of sin whose roots are karma, branches are actions, leaves are sense-organs and flowers are lust. Each metaphor ends with the cry for Narasimha's hand. (Sources: Lakshmi-Nrisimha Karavalamba 3-8; Adi Shankara.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram is auspicious for daily morning recitation in Brahma muhurta and is widely chanted in Vaishnava and Smarta households. It is especially powerful on Thursdays (Guruvar — sacred to Vishnu and Brihaspati), Saturdays (Shanivar — when Narasimha is invoked to pacify Saturn's malefic effects), and Sundays (Ravivar — sacred to the Sun, with whom Narasimha shares fierce solar tejas). Major occasions include Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi, falling at sunset), Swati nakshatra (the birth nakshatra of Narasimha), every Chaturdashi tithi, and during Ekadashi parana. Devotees facing extreme distress, fear of death, persecution by enemies or grave illness traditionally recite it 11 or 21 times until relief is obtained. (Sources: classical Adi Shankara nityapaath tradition; Narasimha sadhana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Karavalamba Stotram bestow according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing 16th verse — Lakshmi-Nrisimha-charana-abja-madhu-vratena, stotram kritam shubhakaram bhuvi Shankarena, ye tat-pathanti manuja Hari-bhakti-yuktah, te yanti tat-pada-sarojam-akhanda-rupam — declares that this auspicious stotra was composed by Shankara as a bee at the lotus feet of Lakshmi-Narasimha, and that Hari-devotees who recite it attain the unbroken (akhanda-rupam) form of Narasimha's lotus feet — that is, moksha. Traditional commentaries extend the benefits to: rescue from every form of samsaric suffering enumerated in the metaphors (fire, net, well, elephant, serpent, tree, ocean, yama-pasha), the combined grace of Lakshmi (wealth, auspiciousness) and Narasimha (protection, fearlessness), removal of fear of death and Yama's messengers, and ultimately the Vaikuntha residence at Narasimha's feet. (Sources: Karavalamba phalashruti; Adi Shankara.)

seoFaqs.question: Which great devotees are remembered in verse 15 of this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 15 of the Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram is a beautiful smarana (remembrance) of fourteen great Vaishnava devotees — the parama-bhagavatas: Prahlada (Hiranyakashipu's son who summoned Narasimha), Narada (the divine sage), Parashara (father of Vyasa), Pundarika (the great devotee of Pandharpur tradition), Vyasa (compiler of the Vedas and Bhagavata), Ambarisha (the king tested by Durvasa), Shuka (narrator of the Bhagavata), Shaunaka (head of the Naimisharanya rishis), Bhishma (the Mahabharata patriarch), Dalbhya, Rukmangada, Arjuna (the Pandava and friend of Krishna), Vasishtha, and Vibhishana (Ravana's brother who took refuge with Rama). By remembering these maha-bhagavatas the devotee invokes their lineage of Hari-bhakti and seeks to join their company. (Sources: Karavalamba verse 15; Bhagavata bhakti tradition.)
